tweets.text:
Personal Computer securely connects to any folder to search, read, and write files locally.

It can access and work across iMessage, Apple Mail, Calendar and other native Mac apps. https://t.co/lbWvJZmpdg

Today we're releasing Personal Computer.

Personal Computer integrates with the Perplexity Mac App for secure orchestration across your local files, native apps, and browser.

We’re rolling this out to all Perplexity Max subscribers and everyone on the waitlist starting today. https://t.co/kxgFQFo7BB

tweets.text:
it follows the best practice from our migration guide:
https://t.co/QnPf7B2OTK

useful Claude Code tip: if you want to test your code w/ Opus 4.7,  just say "migrate to Opus 4.7". the latest release will use our claude-api skill to update model names, prompts, effort, etc specifically for Opus 4.7.
on @ClaudeDevs, we’ll be sharing regular updates aimed at helping developers using Claude, including practical tips + tricks via posts, webinars, and videos from members of our team.
There are plenty of manufacturers that make small and relatively cheap maker devices and chips. Claude Code is *excellent* at programming them into whatever device you can dream up.

You can find the API reference and my example code here: https://t.co/y4keo7bFeU

The API is only enabled if your app is in developer mode. If you're building something cool with it, please share!

Hi! I'm here with *another launch*, it just happens to be extremely niche, nerdy, and probably only for a handful of people. 

In the desktop app, Claude Cowork and Code now have a little Bluetooth API for makers & developers, allowing you to build hardware devices that interact with Claude.

I, for instance, built a little desk pet that alerts me whenever Claude is waiting for permission.
